text: Current state-of-the-art dependency parsers classify based on indicator features, and they generally have millions of such features. These features tend to have poor generalization and feature extraction is a bottleneck in the parsing algorithm. In this paper, we design a transition-based parser using a neural network classifier that is based on a small set of dense features and achieves both higher attachment scores (2% increase in both UAS and LAS on WSJ and Penn Treebank for English and PCCT for Chinese) and higher parsing speed (capable of parsing 1000 sentences per second with 92.2% UAS on WSJ.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
